subject: Cloud Computing
title: Price Wars in the Cloud
description: A 60-second lesson on Price Wars in the Cloud.

hook.text: Between 2006 and 2014, AWS cut prices over 40 times. Google and Azure matched within days.

buildup.text: Cloud providers use price drops as weapons. Lower prices attract volume, and volume reduces unit costs.

discovery.text: This race to the bottom benefits customers. Compute costs have fallen over 90% in a decade.

twist.text: Price wars slowed after 2018. Providers now compete on features, AI, and support — not just price.

climax.text: Commodity cloud is cheap. Premium services like AI, analytics, and security carry much higher margins.

punchline.text: Compute got cheap. The valuable services built on top didn't.

quiz.question: Why did cloud price wars slow down after 2018?
options: Providers shifted competition to features, AI, and support instead of just price
A global agreement fixed cloud prices across all providers
Cloud infrastructure costs stopped decreasing entirely
